l1.label: Leverage Levels
l1.details: Margin $1279.0B (8th consecutive record) | HF gross: record by end-2025, GS PB 3rd straight year record | Multistrat 645% | Debt/GDP 4.06% (ALL-TIME HIGH, exceeds Dot-Com) | Leveraged ETFs $249B

data.notes: GS: gross rose 3rd straight year to record by end-2025. Mag7 ~19% of total US net exposure. Semis net at 5yr high. NOTE: forced derisking Mar 7/10 likely reduced gross — next PB data will confirm, but structural levels remain extreme.

l2.label: Rate of Change
l2.details: Margin YoY +36.5% vs SPX YoY +15.5% = 21pt leverage gap. 8-month streak of record highs. Leverage growth 2.4x market growth — classic late-cycle divergence.

l3.label: Financing Conditions
l3.details: NFCI -0.5236 (loose but tightening — was -0.567 on Feb 6, now 43bp tighter in 5 weeks). HY OAS 3.01% (widened from 2.65% Jan lows — +36bp, approaching stress threshold). SOFR 3.65% (stable). BBB OAS 1.02% (up from 0.93% Jan lows). CCC OAS 9.15%. MOVE 79.74 (down sharply, bond vol easing). Credit stress building but not yet at crisis levels.

l4.label: Positioning Crowding
l4.details: Mag 7 = 42.1% of SPX ($21T). NVDA alone 9.5% ($4.76T). AI Infra Concentration: ~21.4% of SPX across 10 names. HF net short software at 5yr low, net long semis at 5yr high — extreme intra-tech dispersion creates whipsaw risk on any narrative shift.

l5.label: Vol & Options Regime
l5.details: 5A Vol Structure (0.5/1.0): VIX 24.85 (>22 = +0.5). VIX term structure normalizing — front-month pulled back 22% from 31.86 → 24.85. VVIX 122.60 (>110 = +0.5 BUT down sharply from 140.44). Realized > implied still likely given recent moves. 5B Options Sentiment (0.5/1.0): Pipeline P/C 1.187 (EXTREME FEAR, >0.85 = +0.5). 25d risk reversal -8.01 (borderline). GEX flip at 6914, SPX at 6796 = 118pts below flip (NEGATIVE gamma but narrowing fast from 623pts yesterday). VIX 30d RoC: from 16.15 (Jan 26) to 24.85 = +53.9% (>25% = +0.5). Net: 0.5 + 0.5 = 1.0. Key change: VIX crashed 22% intraday, VVIX dropped from 140→123, oil -6.5% on Trump 'war over' comments. Vol regime easing significantly but still elevated.

l6.label: Liquidity Calendar
l6.details: FOMC + Dot Plot Mar 18 (8 days). Quad Witching OPEX Mar 20 (10 days). Q-end rebalancing Mar 31. Cluster is APPROACHING but not within 5-day window yet. Downgraded from 1→0 as FOMC/OPEX moved from 'imminent' (9-11 days) to 'approaching' (8-10 days) — still watchable but not scoring.

score_history.note: SPX below gamma flip, VIX surging
VIX 26.77 (+12.7%), VVIX 125.68, SPX 6753 below 50d/200d MA, deep negative gamma
VIX 31.86, VVIX 140.44, GEX flip 7363 (623pts above spot), GS confirms multistrat-mageddon forced deleveraging Mar 7/10, FOMC in 9 days

VIX 24.85 (-22%), VVIX 122.60 (-12.7%), Oil 88.57 (-6.5% on Trump 'war over'), SPX +0.83% to 6796, GEX flip narrowed to 118pts. Geopolitical de-escalation drove vol compression. Structural leverage unchanged but acute stress easing.

multiplier_effects: L1 (ALL-TIME HIGH leverage) + L3 (tightening but not crisis) = Minsky risk ELEVATED but not triggered today
L4 (Mag7 42.1% concentration) + L5 (GEX 118pts below flip, narrowing) = Amplified unwind risk RECEDING — dealers less short gamma
L5 (VIX 24.85, VVIX 122.60, vol compressing) + L6 (FOMC 8 days, quad witching 10 days) = Calendar risk approaching but vol easing into it
CHANGE: GS PB forced deleveraging from Mar 7/10 appears to be easing as Trump 'war over' comments collapsed oil 6.5% and VIX 22%. The cascade mechanism that was ACTIVE on 3/9 has deactivated for now.
cascade.narrative: EASING FROM PEAK STRESS: The March 10 session represented a sharp reversal from the cascade conditions of March 9. Trump's comments that the Iran war is 'very complete' collapsed oil from $100+ to $88.57 and VIX from 31.86 to 24.85 (-22% single day). The forced deleveraging that GS PB confirmed on March 7/10 appears to be easing — SPX recovered +0.83% to 6796, and the GEX flip point narrowed from 623pts above spot to 118pts. However, structural leverage remains at all-time highs ($1.28T margin, 4.06% debt/GDP), credit is still tightening (HY OAS 3.01%, NFCI -0.524 vs -0.567 five weeks ago), and FOMC + Quad Witching are 8-10 days out. This is a geopolitical de-escalation bounce, not a structural all-clear.

commentary.key_tension: Geopolitical de-escalation (Trump 'war over') drove massive vol compression and risk-on bounce — but structural leverage at all-time highs, credit still tightening, and FOMC/OPEX calendar cluster 8-10 days out means this is relief within an Active Deterioration regime, not a regime change
commentary.bottom_line: Active Deterioration (7.0/10). Downgraded from 9.0 on sharp geopolitical de-escalation: VIX -22% to 24.85, oil -6.5% to $88.57, SPX +0.83%. Forced deleveraging appears easing. But structural leverage at ALL-TIME HIGHS ($1.28T margin, 4.06% debt/GDP, HF gross at record) + credit tightening (HY OAS 3.01%, NFCI tightening 5 straight weeks) + FOMC/Quad Witching in 8-10 days = remain at Active Deterioration. This is a geopolitical relief bounce within structural fragility.

alerts.message: Composite downgraded 9.0 → 7.0 — geopolitical de-escalation drove vol compression. Regime: Active Deterioration.
Margin debt $1.28T (ALL-TIME HIGH), Debt/GDP 4.06% (exceeds Dot-Com), HF gross at record. Forced deleveraging eased acute stress but structural risk persists.
HY OAS 3.01% (+36bp from Jan lows), NFCI tightening 5 straight weeks (-0.567 → -0.524). Credit not confirming 'all clear'.
FOMC + Dot Plot in 8 days, Quad Witching in 10 days — overlapping risk events still ahead.
GEX flip at 6914, SPX at 6796 = 118pts below (vs 623pts yesterday). Negative gamma territory narrowing but not yet positive.
